title: Statement Ingestion technical design
content: # Statement Ingestion technical design
## Outcome

INGEST is a local provenance-and-commit module inside the same .NET 10 Native-AOT `tally` executable as LEDGER. It passively reads one caller-owned PDF from private structured input, detects one of two fixture-qualified text-native layout variants, normalizes exact financial evidence, reconciles every source record, persists an immutable review manifest, and commits only an explicitly approved revision through the versioned public LEDGER operation contract. It has no HTTP surface, daemon, watcher, network dependency, OCR, bank credentials, direct LEDGER storage access, or UI.

The durable INGEST product is the manifest, evidence, approval, idempotency identity, receipt, and recovery protocol. PDF parsers and bank-layout adapters are bounded boundary components rather than a generic document platform.
## Interface exploration
Three public shapes were compared before entity capture.
| Candidate | Shape | Depth and deletion test | Disposition |

| Minimal three-entry surface | `preview`, polymorphic `act`, `status` | Small signature count but shallow: approve, commit, resume, abandon, and cleanup rules move into an action discriminator every caller must understand. Verification still needs every hidden branch. | Rejected. It minimizes names rather than caller knowledge. |

| Explicit workflow operations | `preview`, `inspect`, `approve`, `commit`, `resume`, `status`, `abandon`, `cleanup` | Each transition has one narrow schema, invariant set, error set, and test surface. Removing INGEST would smear parsing, reconciliation, review, overlap, and recovery logic across callers. | Chosen. |

| External process and generic ports | Spawn `tally ledger` and hide parsers, stores, clock, filesystem, and transport behind interfaces | Preserves a black-box seam but adds process orchestration and hypothetical substitutions. Most ports have one implementation; complexity survives deletion as plumbing rather than domain value. | Rejected except for the real two-adapter format seam. |

The INGEST-to-LEDGER boundary uses the same typed operation descriptor and executor that generate the released CLI contract. It does not spawn a child process and does not call a LEDGER feature handler. Format variability is real because two independently qualified adapters exist, so `IStatementAdapter` is justified. PdfPig, SQLite, hashing, serialization, time, and filesystem operations remain concrete until a second production implementation creates a real seam.
## Component shape
One process contains these INGEST zones:
1. `Cli` resolves the eight INGEST operation descriptors, reads versioned JSON through explicit stdin or file input, and maps one result envelope to stdout, stderr, and an exit category.

2. `Contracts/Ingest` owns source-generated request, result, error, manifest-view, receipt, and schema records. Public contracts expose no SQLite, PdfPig, implementation exceptions, source copies, or private LEDGER types.
3. `Features/Ingest` contains vertical slices for Contract, Preview, Review, Commit, and Recovery. Commands own transitions and transactions; queries do not mutate durable state.

4. `Domain/Ingest` owns variant detection outcomes, exact money/date normalization, source-record accounting, reconciliation, manifest canonicalization, overlap policy, and state transitions as deterministic code.
5. `Infrastructure/Ingest/Pdf` contains the concrete PdfPig extractor plus the two explicitly registered format adapters.
6. `Infrastructure/Ingest/Storage` contains the separate raw-SQLite INGEST state store, schema migrations, per-batch commit locks, owner-only artifact checks, and retention compaction.
7. `Integration/Ledger` contains one concrete `LedgerContractClient` that invokes the shared public operation executor with frozen versioned requests and validates returned public results.
8. `Bootstrap/Features/IngestExtensions.cs` explicitly registers descriptors, handlers, concrete services, and exactly the two qualified adapters. No reflection scanning or plugin loading is used.
## Public operation contract

All complex inputs use `--input -` or `--input @file`; `sourcePath` is accepted only inside the private JSON input and never as a command-line argument. Exactly one business envelope is emitted to stdout. Diagnostics are metadata-only on stderr.
| CLI command | Stable operation | Responsibility |

| `tally ingest preview` | `ingest.preview` | Read and fingerprint one source, validate the selected account, detect/extract/normalize/reconcile, evaluate replay and overlap, and persist one immutable manifest revision without LEDGER mutation. |
| `tally ingest inspect` | `ingest.inspect` | Return the sensitive owner-only manifest view for one exact revision. |
| `tally ingest approve` | `ingest.approve` | Record explicit approval of one committable manifest digest and actor without mutation. |
| `tally ingest commit` | `ingest.commit` | Commit unresolved accepted candidates in frozen order through public LEDGER operations. |
| `tally ingest resume` | `ingest.resume` | Acquire the batch lock and replay only unresolved frozen requests with their original idempotency keys. |

| `tally ingest status` | `ingest.status` | Return safe metadata for one batch, or a cursor page ordered by updatedAt descending then batchId with default 50 and maximum 100 retained-batch summaries when no batch is supplied. |
| `tally ingest abandon` | `ingest.abandon` | Make an incomplete batch terminal, preserve prior LEDGER effects, and compact disposable sensitive state. |
| `tally ingest cleanup` | `ingest.cleanup` | Remove only a completed metadata receipt or safely abandoned artifacts after state and lock checks. |

There is no generic import, run, save, update, delete, parser configuration, SQL, or database operation. Supported variants and all schemas, limits, errors, and exit categories come from the shared operation registry and system schema commands.
## Preview and reconciliation flow
1. Validate contract versions, source-path schema, selected account identity, and advertised limits before opening the source.
2. Query the active account and LEDGER contract through the public operation executor.

3. Open the caller-owned file read-only, capture safe before/after metadata, read at most the advertised byte limit into one immutable buffer, and compute SHA-256 over exactly those bytes. Any observed change blocks preview.
4. PdfPig 0.1.14 extracts ordered pages, glyph values, and coordinates in-process. No links, annotations, embedded objects, scripts, rendering, remote fetch, or OCR are executed.
5. Every registered adapter probes the same immutable evidence. Exactly one exact structural match is required; zero or multiple matches fail closed.

6. The selected adapter produces ordered source-record evidence and variant controls. Every source record receives exactly one candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked disposition.

7. Normalize ZAR integer minor units, owner-economic sign, transaction date, optional distinct posting date, original description, selected account, source reference, and statement-import provenance. Any ambiguity blocks the record and batch.
8. Reconcile opening plus movements to closing and every available running balance or source control exactly. Unavailable controls are recorded as unavailable.

9. Detect exact replay first from selected account and source fingerprint. A prior active or completed batch returns its prior manifest, status, or receipt without reparsing or mutation; original version compatibility governs whether an incomplete batch may resume. Only when no exact replay exists does a different source with an overlapping account/date window reach overlap evaluation, where the initial adapters provide no cross-file identity and therefore emit a blocking conflict.

10. Canonicalize and hash the complete ordered manifest, then persist batch, revision, records, candidates, controls, and failure-safe status in one INGEST SQLite transaction. No LEDGER mutation occurs.
## Identity, review, commit, and resume
- `batchId` is a stable SHA-256-derived identifier over selected account, source fingerprint, adapter identity/version, manifest schema version, and compatible LEDGER contract version.
- `manifestRevisionId` is the digest of canonical source-generated JSON for the complete ordered immutable manifest.

- `sourceRecordId` is a stable hash over source fingerprint, page/section/transaction-record ordinal, raw record-evidence fingerprint, and the immutable-facts schema. It deliberately excludes adapter, manifest, executable, and compatible contract versions. It distinguishes identical date-description-amount rows at different positions but makes no cross-file claim.

- The two initial adapters leave cross-file transaction identity absent. Tuple similarity never suppresses a candidate. Exact source replay is automatic; other overlapping source windows block under OQ-INGEST-4.

- `candidateId` is derived from selected account, sourceRecordId, and immutable normalized facts; it deliberately excludes manifest revision and compatible version metadata. The LEDGER idempotency key and statement-import source reference are derived from that stable candidate identity, so re-review cannot create a second canonical transaction.

- Approval names the exact batch, manifest revision, manifest digest, actor attribution, and trusted OS identity. Any account, adapter, contract, disposition, or fact change creates a new preview and invalidates earlier approval for commit.

- The frozen LEDGER request is persisted before commit. A per-batch OS lock prevents concurrent commit/resume processes. Before each call, INGEST durably marks the candidate unresolved/attempting; after the public LEDGER result and transaction-detail round trip, it durably records the terminal receipt before continuing.

- A crash after LEDGER commit but before receipt durability is closed by replaying the same request and idempotency key. A changed or incompatible contract, mismatched canonical reference, or conflicting replay blocks continuation.
## Persistence and retention

INGEST owns a separate `ingest.db` beneath the owner-only Tally data root. It never opens Ledger storage. Raw Microsoft.Data.Sqlite uses foreign keys, WAL, `synchronous=FULL`, bounded busy handling, immutable ordered migrations through `PRAGMA user_version`, and explicit SQL transactions. The separate state store preserves C2, allows INGEST-specific retention, and makes the cross-store boundary honest; safety across the two stores comes from the public LEDGER idempotency contract rather than a fictitious distributed transaction.

Incomplete manifests and receipts retain the sensitive data needed for review or resume. After every candidate is terminal and canonical references are verified, INGEST atomically compacts the full manifest, candidate requests, source descriptions, amounts, and reconciliation evidence into a completed metadata-only receipt containing stable identifiers, versions, outcome categories, canonical references, and counts. Completed metadata receipts remain until explicit cleanup. Abandonment retains only the metadata tombstone needed to prevent resume. An interrupted batch may be explicitly abandoned after its process lock is released; prior LEDGER effects remain canonical and are summarized in the tombstone. Safe startup cleanup removes only unlocked stale INGEST lock or atomic-publication artifacts after reconciling them with durable state. The caller-owned source is never copied, relocated, changed, or deleted. Extraction is memory-only; normal operation creates no extraction temporary.
## Security and resource contract
- Data root/directories are 0700 and INGEST database, WAL, SHM, lock, and atomic artifacts are 0600 where supported; inability to establish protection blocks sensitive persistence.

- Source paths, rows, descriptions, amounts, balances, full bank identifiers, requests, manifests, and receipts never enter process arguments, logs, stderr, crash diagnostics, or telemetry. Sensitive business results are permitted only on the explicit owner-invoked stdout contract.
- Source and manifest serialization use source-generated JSON with unknown fields rejected. Canonical hashing never relies on reflection-based property order.
- PdfPig is pinned and its extraction-policy version is part of adapter compatibility. Every upgrade must republish Native-AOT and replay the qualified golden fixtures before support changes.

- Each adapter advertises byte, page, record, nesting, decompression, memory, and wall-time limits. Cancellation propagates through every asynchronous boundary; over-limit and malformed sources fail before mutation.
- The sole-owner OS identity is attribution and local access control, not application authentication. A daemon, remote client, hosted service, or multi-user mode requires a new security design.
## Feature boundaries
- Contract and Formats: descriptors, schemas, version compatibility, PdfPig extraction, adapter registry, and qualified variant descriptors.
- Preview and Reconciliation: source snapshot, normalization, record accounting, controls, exact replay, overlap, and immutable manifests.
- Review and Commit: inspect, approval, frozen LEDGER requests, public contract invocation, and terminal receipts.
- Recovery and Cleanup: status, batch locks, resume, abandonment, compaction, permissions, migrations, and cleanup.

INGEST has a hard runtime dependency on LEDGER and no dependency on CLASSIFY, BUDGET, INSIGHTS, or CHANNELS. Optional classification is a later explicit caller action after canonical commit. LEDGER has no dependency on INGEST.
## CORE compliance matrix
| Design axis | INGEST obligation or explicit non-applicability |
| Save/upsert | Omitted. Preview, approve, commit, resume, abandon, and cleanup are narrow state-transition commands. |
| Get detail | `ingest.inspect` returns the exact sensitive manifest; `ingest.status` returns safe workflow state. |
| GetGridList | No grid or UI. Status without a batch returns bounded retained-batch summaries. |
| GetLookupList | No UI lookup. Qualified variant descriptors are published through schema discovery. |
| Delete/bulk delete | No generic delete. Cleanup is state-checked; active recovery data is RESTRICTed. |
| Domain endpoints | Represented as typed CLI operations, not HTTP endpoints. |
| DTOs/frontend key models | Source-generated versioned records live in `Contracts/Ingest`; frontend models do not exist in this module. |
| Commands/queries | Each public operation has one explicit handler. Inspect/status are queries; preview/approve/commit/resume/abandon/cleanup are commands. |
| DTOMapper/EntityMapper | Concrete stateless boundary, manifest, and SQLite row mappers. Mappers never query, mutate state, or own business rules. No EF `EntityMapperResult`. |
| DbContext factory | Not applicable: the local CLI deliberately uses raw SQLite and explicit caller-owned transactions. |

| Permissions/roles/seeds | No application users, tenants, permissions, role templates, nav/menu seeds, translations, or business seed data. Adapter descriptors are explicit code registration, not configurable seed data. |
| Persona verification | Published-binary process tests exercise P1 and AI Agent Host flows using private fixture injection; fixtures never ship or enter the graph. |

| UI ownership | NG8 excludes INGEST UI. No UI delivery is promised. CHANNELS may later consume the public contract only after its own future PRD/design; INGEST has no mockups, components, services, routes, translations, or browser tests. |
## Inherited prior-art disposition
- ADR-CORE-0010 CommandResult: adapted to stable result/error plus exit category.
- ADR-CORE-0021 explicit DI and ADR-CORE-0024 vertical slices: adopted.
- ADR-CORE-0025 stable error strategy: adopted without HTTP ProblemDetails.
- ADR-CORE-0004 enums: adopted for closed workflow, disposition, outcome, control, and error categories.
- ADR-CORE-0020 RESTRICT delete: adopted for incomplete/recoverable INGEST state; terminal artifacts have explicit cleanup.
- ADR-CORE-0022 mapping intent: adapted to concrete pure boundary/row mappers.
- ADR-CORE-0016, ADR-CORE-0018, and ADR-CORE-0027: not applicable because INGEST uses raw SQLite, snake_case identifiers, and no EF Core or hosted request pipeline.
- FastEndpoints, HTTP, Aspire, PostgreSQL, RLS, tenancy, Angular, grid DTOs, permissions, menus, translations, and frontend patterns are rejected by C1, C2, NG7, and NG8.
## Test architecture

- Pure unit tests cover adapter detection exclusivity, row identity, money/date normalization, source-record accounting, exact controls, canonical manifest hashing, state transitions, retention, and overlap fail-closed rules.
- Adapter integration tests run the real PdfPig dependency and both concrete adapters against injected private golden fixtures; fixture bytes and expected private facts remain outside source control.
- Raw-SQLite integration tests use real temporary stores for migrations, constraints, immutable revisions, approvals, compaction, batch locks, and candidate receipts. SQL is not mocked.
- Cross-module contract tests invoke the shared public LEDGER operation executor and published binary; private handlers and databases are inaccessible to the test.
- Published-binary tests cover schema snapshots, private stdin source paths, stdout/stderr isolation, all exit categories, offline operation, Native-AOT trimming, no orphan process, and cancellation.
- Fault injection covers every boundary before/after attempting a LEDGER call and before/after receipt durability. Repeated resume must converge without a second canonical transaction.

- Security/performance tests assert permissions, source hashes, canary absence from arguments/diagnostics, zero extraction temporary, under-5-second preview, and under-256-MiB RSS for every qualified fixture.
## Planned implementation locations
The repository is greenfield, so these are planning obligations rather than current files:
- `src/Tally/Tally.csproj`
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ingest`
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Contract`
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Preview`
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Review`
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Commit`
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Recovery`
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ingest`
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Pdf`
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Storage`
- `src/Tally/Integration/Ledger`
- `src/Tally/Bootstrap/Features/IngestExtensions.cs`
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest`

Lex 0.5.3 treats truthful greenfield `expected_paths` as present-time missing-path warnings. As in MD-LEDGER-MASTER, the exact paths are canonical here and must become task acceptance criteria in planning; decision metadata is not populated until the implementation creates those paths.
## Evidence and remaining gate

OQ-INGEST-11 is resolved: a throwaway published .NET 10 Native-AOT spike using upstream PdfPig 0.1.14 extracted all three qualified fixtures twice with identical digests. The worst observed invocation was 0.37 seconds and 51,416 KiB RSS, within the 5-second/256-MiB target. The legacy package failed after trimming and is forbidden.

OQ-INGEST-4 remains open for future overlapping or corrected-statement evidence. It does not make v1 unsafe: the two initial adapters claim exact source replay only, never tuple or cross-file identity, and block any different source whose account/date window overlaps retained evidence.
origin: Approved INGEST PRD, inherited CORE graph, LEDGER technical design, private fixture evidence, and OQ-INGEST-11 PdfPig Native-AOT spike
